mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL paired with a flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers expected real rates and discount-rate pressure on equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation periods do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the interaction term loses predictive sign in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to raise recession odds, which lowers S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-surprise trigger events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average conditional return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger household income and spending power, which supports revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvements fail to outperform single-series signals, or if the combined signal is associated with negative 20d SPX returns in a majority of expanding-demand episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL signals improving real output without overheating, which should support earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO with non-accelerating CPI do not lead to above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal loses sign stability across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% tightens financial conditions enough to pressure equity valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days should be negative while the rate remains above that threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes produce non-negative 5d SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or if the conditional average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly drop in GDPC1 relative to its prior reading indicates demand contraction and weaker earnings growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more than half the time, or if the out-of-sample rank correlation remains near zero for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor demand is improving without slack buildup, which should boost confidence in cyclicals and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down combination does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered episodes fail to produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST alongside stable INDPRO signals rebuilding activity and supply normalization, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if recovery episodes with rising HOUST and stable-to-rising INDPRO fail to generate positive 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is not statistically distinct from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.
